failure_story: We sized Simplex Financial Holdings (7176.T) as a Japan ETF and asset-management croupier, treating a mechanical 38–53% annual return as actionable despite a watch stance, illiquid TSE tape, and peak-cycle success fees. Entry assumed either the January 2026 last print (¥464, 100 shares) or a Yahoo feed quote of ¥757 that exactly matches FY2026 book value per share (¥757.01), conflating accounting book with an investable market price. FY2026 parent EPS had already surged to ¥317.33 (+131% YoY) on success fees of ¥14.316B (+53.6% YoY), roughly 65% of combined base and performance fees, while our Lawrence anchor used FY2025 mid-cycle owner cash of ¥137.14 per share. When domestic equity markets mean-reverted and performance fees collapsed toward the FY2025 run-rate, consolidated parent net income fell from ¥10.6B toward ¥7B or lower, invalidating both the peak earnings narrative and the component reserve haircut of only −¥140 per share. We could not build or exit at modeled prices: Yahoo showed only two volume days above zero in twelve months, Stooq had one usable daily row, and any attempt to accumulate moved the thin float. The scarcity premium embedded in sporadic prints deflated as earnings normalized, producing simultaneous earnings miss and multiple compression. Hold-period return landed near or below the bear scenario (28.2% Lawrence / 43.0% legacy dive bear on normalized cash) versus the 50.3% Lawrence base and 53.33% synthesis headline—severe underperformance on a fee-cyclical manager sized for croupier durability at the wrong point in the performance-fee cycle while execution risk was explicitly flagged but overridden.
earliest_warning_signals: Watch stance in classification and deep dive explicitly cited illiquid TSE tape and peak success-fee year; human_review.live_price_confirmed was false in valuation.json.
trading_liquidity.status illiquid with only two Yahoo volume days in twelve months (2025-07-03 and 2026-01-07) and last print 100 shares at ¥464—not VWAP or investable depth.
FY2026 success fees ¥14.316B (+53.6% YoY) were ~65% of combined base + performance fees; mid-cycle anchor excluded FY2026 peak ¥317.33 EPS in favor of FY2025 ¥137.14.
Yahoo price ¥757 (2026-07-23) equals BVPS ¥757.01 from finfo2026—likely vendor mislabeling of book value as market price; IRR math used conflicting entry prints (¥464 vs ¥757).
Milly flagged executive summary 53.33% vs valuation.json base 38.04% (tol 0.25pp)—headline return and stance gate diverged at decision time.

Payoff gate 5 failed: no dated predictive attribute; mispricing thesis rested on illiquidity and non-continuous price, not a catalyst with timing.
Dhando partial with no book floor: asset-light manager; bear case earnings compress with markets without NAV support.
Holdco structure: parent entity shows ~¥18M revenue and operating loss per management_facts; consolidated profit from subsidiaries not field-verified in yuho.
Nov 2025 1-for-20 split broke earlier placeholder share-count math (~300M pre-split); split-adjusted EPS only reliable filing path.
Component additive base ~¥2,264 per share vs last print ¥464 implied deep discount only if print is investable—discount illusory when tape cannot fill.
Short activist scan batch no_hit only (short_scan_2026-05-28.md); adversarial pass flagged Tier-1 web scan as [HUMAN REVIEW], not completed before sizing.

forensic_checks: Confirm live TSE quote and depth on broker terminal before any sizing; reconcile against Yahoo/Stooq feeds and flag if quote equals BVPS (¥757.01 FY2026) rather than last trade (¥464 Jan 2026, 100 shares).

Quarterly bridge base fees vs success fees from Pnotice variance notices and finfo2026 earnings releases; test whether normalized owner cash reverts below ¥137.14 per share for two consecutive quarters per performance_fee_cycle_reserve falsifier.
Download and read yuho via EDINET E31267; verify segment note, related-party flows, subsidiary dividend upstream, and non-recurring items not visible in 決算短信.
Reconcile operating cash flow (¥11.694B FY2026) vs parent net income (¥10.631B) for owner-cash normalization; document holdco vs consolidated cash path.

Sum four additive components (core_fee_engine ¥2,239.37, etf_product_pipeline ¥100.00, net_financial_claims ¥65.03, performance_fee_cycle_reserve −¥140.02) versus actual entry price and test low/base/high contract returns.
Monitor AUM (¥1,335.7B FY2026, +2.9% YoY) vs fee revenue growth (+38.5% revenue); flag fee yield expansion driven by performance fees rather than durable AUM growth.
Track leveraged/inverse ETF regulatory actions in Japan; quantify AUM and fee exposure if product lines face restriction.
Reverse DCF at entry price: what owner-cash level and exit multiple are required to earn 15% annual return with mid-cycle ¥137.14 starting cash and base 16× year-10 exit?
Verify post-split issued shares (27.12M per evidence reconciliation) and weighted average (33.5M FY2026) against latest filings before reinvestment DCF refresh.
Run Tier-1 short activist registry and targeted forensic web scan; no local short_reports/ and only batch no_hit on record at packet freeze.
Compare Darwin/monthly returns vault (7176_T.csv mostly 0% months) against business fundamentals to detect false backtest inclusion.
Read English translations in research/evidence/_text_en/ for governance, capital actions, and shareholder meeting claims; verify management_facts diversification and breakeven excerpts.
